sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
sections.passage: Have you ever heard of scorpion fish? I was completely (1) ...... they existed until I trod on one in the sea. I can't (2) ...... enough how painful it was! The fish has a spike which gives you an (3) ...... of a (4) ...... substance. It's not dangerous, but you begin to feel extremely (5) ...... , and the pain just gets worse and worse. Luckily, there was a doctor on the beach – she was a (6) ...... at the local hospital – and she told me what the best (7) ...... was. I had to bathe my foot in warm, salty water and then apply an ammonia-based solution. After my holiday, my foot still seemed to be a little infected, so my local GP gave me a (8) ...... for antibiotics. Unfortunately, it turned out I was (9) ...... to them, so I came out in a red rash all over my body. It was over a week before I had made a complete (10) ...... .

sections.title: B: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:

Thankfully, there ...... to have an operation.

Your arm ...... heal before the match on Saturday.

I'd love ...... a hundred press-ups in one go.

Do you think I should reduce the amount of chocolate I eat? **on**

sections.title: C: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold
sections.instructions: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold.
